claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 6 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! I can see you have a 6-year-old daughter named Alex who lives with you.

To properly assess your eligibility, I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand a bit more about your situation. 

**Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for her daily needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for her care - I provide all her daily needs like food, clothes, and a home. She lives with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation provided by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, she was in care for a short time - just a couple of months earlier this year - but she's back home with me now and living with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that. I need to understand this situation better as it may affect eligibility.

**How long exactly was Alex in care, and does she still spend any regular time away from home (for example, going back to stay with a carer or spending certain days/nights elsewhere)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She was in care for exactly 7 weeks. And no, she doesn't spend any time away from home anymore - she's back living with me completely now and doesn't go anywhere else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful information.

Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as the other parent or another relative?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her. It's just me looking after her and I'm the only one who would be claiming.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Do you have any immigration status considerations I should be aware of? For example, do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen with full right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen with full right to reside in the UK, so there are no immigration issues.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✓ Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
✓ You live in the UK and are a British citizen
✓ You are responsible for Alex's care and she lives with you full-time
✓ You provide for all her needs (food, clothes, accommodation)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

Regarding the time Alex spent in care: Since it was only 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week threshold that would affect Child Benefit payments. Now that she's back home with you full-time, you can proceed with your claim.

**Next Steps:**
You can now make a claim for Child Benefit. The claim process is straightforward, and you should be able to apply online or by post.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the key criteria: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (she is 6 years old), (2) the claimant lives in the UK and is a British citizen with full right to reside, (3) the claimant is responsible for Alex's care - providing food, clothes, and accommodation, (4) Alex lives with the claimant full-time, (5) no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and (6) although Alex was in care, it was only for 7 weeks, which is under the 8-week threshold after which Child Benefit payments would normally be affected. Since Alex is now back home living with the claimant full-time, the claimant can claim Child Benefit for her.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 6-year-old daughter Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for her care and is a British citizen, and no one else is claiming for her. The brief 7-week period in care is under the 8-week threshold and Alex is now back home full-time, so this does not affect eligibility.

Since Alex is now back home living with the claimant full-time, the claimant can claim Child Benefit for her.

The brief 7-week period in care is under the 8-week threshold and Alex is now back home full-time, so this does not affect eligibility.
